text.title: Function Calling: Concepts and Provider Formats
text.summary: Function calling flow, tool choice modes, provider format differences (OpenAI/Claude/Gemini), and the agentic loop. The LLM never executes functions — it produces a call specification; your code executes it.

list.item: Context Assembly: system message + tool definitions + user message → LLM input
Intent Analysis: LLM decides if a tool call is needed
Tool Selection: LLM picks tool(s) and extracts parameters as JSON
Execution: your code runs the actual function with validated arguments
Result Processing: function result appended to context → LLM generates final response

rule.statement: Never pass raw LLM-generated arguments directly to functions without validation.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate parameter values (e.g., wrong IDs, invented enums). Structured error returns with field-level messages let the model self-correct on retry.

list.item: auto — LLM decides whether to call tools; use for general conversations
required (OpenAI) / any (Claude) — LLM must call at least one tool; use for extraction tasks
none — tools disabled for this call; use for follow-up questions
specific tool name — force a named tool; use for deterministic workflows

rule.statement: Keep the tool catalogue to ≤20 per call. Use RAG-over-tools to select the relevant subset for larger catalogues.
rule.rationale: Each tool definition consumes input tokens on every call. Beyond ~20 tools, selection accuracy degrades. Dynamic selection scales to 100+ tools without accuracy loss.

rule.statement: OpenAI uses tools/tool_calls; Claude uses tools/tool_use content blocks; Gemini uses function_declarations/function_call.
rule.rationale: Providers share the concept but differ in field names and message structure. Use an abstraction layer or provider-specific branches when targeting multiple providers.

example.note: OpenAI tool definition and response handling
Claude tool definition and response handling

rule.statement: Always set a max_iterations guard (10–20) on every agentic loop to prevent infinite execution.
rule.rationale: Without an iteration cap, a malformed tool result or persistent LLM confusion causes unbounded token spend and external API calls.

list.item: Single tool call: User → LLM → one tool → execute → response
Parallel calls: LLM returns multiple tool_calls simultaneously → execute all → aggregate results → response
Sequential (agentic): LLM → tool A → result → LLM → tool B → result → … → response
Tool router: router LLM selects tool subset → specialist LLM executes → response; best for >20 tools
Parallel tool calls: OpenAI Yes, Claude Yes, Gemini Yes, Local (Ollama) model-dependent
Streaming tool calls: OpenAI Yes, Claude Yes, Gemini Yes, Local limited
Max tools per call: OpenAI 128, Claude 64, Gemini 128, Local model-dependent
Interleaved thinking: Claude only (extended thinking mode)
Structured output: OpenAI JSON mode + tools, Claude tools only, Gemini JSON mode + tools

text.reference: Validated tool dispatcher with registry decorator and structured error return
Example tool definitions in OpenAI and Claude format
